title: chained Vec<&Form> extraction is awkward abstraction
description: extract_match_type, extract_comparator, and extract_address_part all take &[&Form] and return (T, Vec<&Form>) so they can be chained. This pattern forces the caller to write three lines of let-binding and results in three allocations for what is conceptually a single scan of a short slice (evaluator.rs:546-549, 569-573, 595-598). Suggested rewrite: a single extract_test_opts() function that takes &[Form] and returns a struct { match_type, comparator, address_part, remaining: &[Form] } via one linear scan, keeping remaining as a slice view (or start/end indices) rather than an owned Vec.
